epicsWorkbench.runtime.defaultProtocol.description: Default protocol to prefer when creating runtime monitors.

epicsWorkbench.runtime.channelCreationTi.description: Timeout in seconds for creating a runtime monitoring channel. Set to 0 to keep epics-tca searching and reconnecting until the channel is found.

epicsWorkbench.runtime.monitorSubscribeT.description: Timeout in seconds for the initial runtime monitor subscription. Set to 0 to keep the subscription open for epics-tca recovery and re-subscribe handling.

epicsWorkbench.runtime.logLevel.description: epics-tca log level used by the runtime monitoring context.

epicsWorkbench.runtime.environment.description: Environment variable overrides passed to the epics-tca runtime context, for example EPICS_CA_ADDR_LIST or EPICS_PVA_ADDR_LIST.

epicsWorkbench.tdm.rootPath.description: Path to a local TDM checkout or installation root used by `externalBinary` mode. If empty, EPICS Workbench tries TDM_ROOT and a sibling ../tdm folder automatically.

epicsWorkbench.tdm.runtimeMode.description: How EPICS Workbench launches TDM. `vendoredSource` runs the copied runtime under `vscode/vendor/tdm`; `externalBinary` uses a separately built TDM executable.

epicsWorkbench.tdm.executablePath.description: Optional path to a runnable TDM executable used by `externalBinary` mode. Use this when the default packaged or Electron-based TDM launch path cannot be discovered automatically.

epicsWorkbench.tdm.profilesPath.description: Optional path to the TDM profiles.json file. If empty, EPICS Workbench uses ~/.tdm/profiles.json.

epicsWorkbench.tdm.profile.description: TDM profile name to use for the embedded display runtime. If empty, EPICS Workbench picks the first non-'For All Profiles' profile from the configured profiles file.

epicsWorkbench.tdm.httpServerPort.description: Web-mode port for the TDM subprocess. Set to 0 to let EPICS Workbench choose a free local port automatically.
